g.title:
Esri Feature Layer
The source of truth. Can be hosted on ArcGIS Online or your own ArcGIS Enterprise portal. Supports public layers (no auth) and private layers (username/password token auth). 2FA-protected accounts are not supported — use a service account.
Records are fetched via the Feature Layer REST API /query endpoint with pagination (up to 1000 records per page).

REST API Poll
HTTP GET to the Feature Layer /query endpoint every N seconds (configurable poll interval). Requests all fields in WGS84 coordinates, up to 1000 records per page with automatic pagination. When delta tracking is enabled, only records modified since the last run are fetched — reducing bandwidth and TAK Server load.

config.json
Stored at /opt/Esri-TAKServer-Sync/config.json Defines: TAK Server host/port, auth mode (cert or plain TCP), Feature Layer URL, field mapping (lat/lon/callsign/cot_type), delta tracking settings, icon mapping rules. Written by infra-TAK on each deploy or Config tab save.

delta-state.json
Stored at /opt/Esri-TAKServer-Sync/delta-state.json When delta mode is on, only records with an EditDate newer than the stored value are sent. This reduces bandwidth and TAK Server load significantly for large layers. Delete this file to force a full re-broadcast on next start.

feature-layer-to-cot.py
Python worker script, runs as a systemd service (feature-layer-to-cot.service). Log: /var/log/esri-takserver-sync-feature-layer-to-cot.log (truncated on each restart)
Each poll cycle: 1. Fetches all (or changed) records from the Feature Layer 2. Maps lat/lon/callsign/cot_type fields to CoT attributes 3. Resolves icon path from icon mapping table (if enabled) 4. Builds a CoT XML event element for each record 5. Streams to TAK Server over persistent TCP/TLS connection 6. Sends keepalive ping every 15s to hold the connection open

icons/*.zip — Icon Mapping
Iconset .zip files stored in /opt/Esri-TAKServer-Sync/icons/ Each zip must contain iconset.xml with a uid attribute.
The Icons tab in infra-TAK lets you map a column value to an iconset path. The poller injects: <usericon iconsetpath="uuid/Group/icon.png"/> into each CoT event so ATAK/WinTAK renders the correct icon.
Upload zips via the web UI — they save directly to the server.

TLS/Cert Mode — port 8089 (recommended)
Uses mTLS — client presents a .p12 certificate signed by TAK Server's CA. TAK Server validates the cert against its trusted CA list.
Generate cert: cd /opt/tak/certs ./makeCert.sh Client esri-push sudo java -jar /opt/tak/utils/UserManager.jar certmod -A /opt/tak/certs/files/esri-push.p12
Extract PEM sidecars (note the -legacy flag for RC2 encryption): openssl pkcs12 -legacy -in esri-push.p12 -nokeys -out esri-push-cert.pem openssl pkcs12 -legacy -in esri-push.p12 -nocerts -nodes -out esri-push-key.pem
Do NOT set ca_cert unless your server cert CN matches the hostname — causes SSL hostname mismatch errors.

Plain TCP Mode — port 8087
Unencrypted TCP connection — simpler to set up but traffic is not encrypted. Only use on a private/trusted network.
TAK Server must have a plain TCP input configured in CoreConfig.xml. Many default TAK Server installs only have TLS enabled on 8089 and will silently drop plain TCP data even if the connection is accepted.

TAK Server
Receives the CoT XML stream and fans it out to all connected clients in the configured group.
The esri-push user/cert must be enrolled in a TAK group (e.g. __ANON__ or a custom group). All clients subscribed to that group will see the PLIs.
Stale time is configurable per CoT event — records disappear from maps after N minutes if not refreshed by a new poll cycle.

CoT Distribution
TAK Server sends CoT events to all connected ATAK, WinTAK, iTAK, and TAK Web clients. Each Feature Layer record appears as a PLI (Position Location Information) marker on the map with its callsign label and — if icon mapping is configured — a custom icon and colour.

ATAK — Android Team Awareness Kit
Feature Layer records appear as PLI markers on the ATAK map. Custom icons render if the matching iconset UUID is installed on the device (install via ATAK Preferences > Manage Iconsets). Remarks fields appear in the detail callout when tapping a marker.

WinTAK — Windows Team Awareness Kit
Same CoT stream as ATAK. The bundled Incident Icons iconset is pre-installed in WinTAK by default — icons from that set render immediately without any additional setup on the client. Custom iconsets must be installed separately on each WinTAK device.

TAK Web / iTAK / TAKX
Any CoT-compatible client connected to the same TAK Server and enrolled in the same group will receive Feature Layer records in real time. This includes browser-based TAK Web, iOS iTAK, and any third-party CoT consumer.
